note:
{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment aims to detect the side population (SP) using flow cytometry with Hoechst-stained mouse bone marrow cells. The goal is to effectively identify SP cells by analyzing their fluorescence under high-power lasers at 375 nm and 405 nm.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "A scientist in a lab coat and gloves prepares samples for analysis.",
    "The scientist uses a computer interface to control and analyze the flow cytometer data.",
    "Data from the flow cytometer is displayed on a computer screen, showing histograms and scatter plots of cell populations."
  ],
  "materials_and_subjects": [
    "Mouse bone marrow cells stained with Hoechst dye.",
    "High-power lasers at 375 nm and 405 nm.",
    "Computer software for data analysis."
  ],
  "tools_and_setup": [
    "Flow cytometer equipped with high-power lasers.",
    "Computer with specialized software for data analysis.",
    "Lab environment with appropriate safety gear and equipment."
  ],
  "quantitative_observations": [
    "Histograms and scatter plots displaying cell populations.",
    "Laser power settings: 20 mW for 375 nm and 80 mW for 405 nm.",
    "Percentage of SP cells detected: 0.29% for 375 nm and 0.33% for 405 nm."
  ],
  "key_transitions": [
    "Transition from sample preparation to data collection and analysis on the computer."
  ],
  "outcomes_or_indicators": [
    "Representative results displayed on the computer screen.",
    "Detection of SP cells based on fluorescence intensity and laser power."
  ],
  "anything_unusual_or_notable": [
    "Use of high-power lasers for fluorescence detection.",
    "Comparison of SP cell detection rates between different laser wavelengths."
